scope: Proof artifacts Daxis must provide before Axon can be treated as production default browser read compute.
owner: Daxis product, platform, storage, catalog, web, security, and SRE owners

externalItems.summary: Daxis architecture and product docs name Axon as browser read compute and remove stale DuckDB-WASM terminology for this path.
requiredProofArtifacts: Daxis architecture doc links or commit SHAs
acceptanceChecks: Docs name Axon as browser read compute
Docs keep Daxis as policy, auth, audit, and fallback owner
Docs do not imply browser-owned credentials or signing
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Docs include server fallback as the default rollback path for unsupported browser execution.

externalItems.summary: Daxis product architecture names Axon as the default browser read engine before default routing is claimed.
requiredProofArtifacts: Daxis product architecture reference naming Axon as the default browser read engine
Rollout or release-channel approval showing the default engine claim is intentional
Owner signoff for server fallback as the rollback path
acceptanceChecks: Default-engine wording is tied to supported interactive browser reads, not all Daxis compute
The architecture reference preserves Daxis ownership of policy, audit, access grants, and fallback
The rollout state can still use integration or candidate channels before stable_default
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Daxis architecture and rollout docs describe how a segment returns to server_fallback if Axon cannot remain the default browser path.

externalItems.owner: Daxis gateway, catalog, and storage platform
externalItems.summary: Daxis implements the descriptor resolver endpoint and proves descriptor success and fail-closed behavior.
requiredProofArtifacts: Signed URL or proxy URL scoping evidence
Expiry, snapshot mismatch, path escape, and malformed descriptor negative test output
Request and correlation ID propagation logs
acceptanceChecks: Resolver returns BrowserHttpSnapshotDescriptor-compatible payloads
Resolver never returns raw cloud credentials
Resolver preserves requested snapshot rules
Resolver errors preserve structured code and correlation ID
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Daxis can route affected tenants or table classes to server_fallback while preserving the resolver failure reason.

externalItems.summary: Daxis UI opens a governed table through the Axon SDK, queries it, handles fallback/block states, and preserves request IDs.
requiredProofArtifacts: Daxis UI fallback and block rendering test output
Worker command capture showing no credential profile identifiers or secrets
Browser matrix result for the supported rollout browsers
acceptanceChecks: Daxis UI calls Daxis endpoints rather than private Axon internals
Daxis UI passes descriptors or read plans through the public Axon SDK
Daxis UI preserves fallback and block reasons internally
Daxis UI can cancel or roll back to server execution
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Daxis feature flag or rollout control can disable browser execution for the affected segment.

externalItems.summary: Daxis implements read-access-plan outcomes for brokered_delta, delta_sharing, sql_fallback_required, and blocked.
requiredProofArtifacts: Policy denied and policy unknown test output
Correlation logs from gateway, catalog, and query service
acceptanceChecks: Endpoint returns closed read-access-plan variants
Endpoint preserves policy and fallback reasons
Endpoint does not expose direct catalog tokens or raw credentials
Endpoint can return server fallback without opening the Axon worker
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Daxis can return sql_fallback_required for affected table classes or tenants.

externalItems.owner: Daxis storage platform and security engineering
externalItems.summary: Daxis validates signed URL CORS and proxy mode against the production endpoint shape.
requiredProofArtifacts: Production XML endpoint CORS validation output
Allowed origin, allowed method, exposed header, and range-read evidence
URL redaction evidence for logs and UI events
acceptanceChecks: Signed URL reads expose required range headers to the Daxis origin
Proxy mode enforces grant and path scope
CORS failure can switch the table or segment to proxy or server fallback
Signed URLs are object-scoped and short-lived
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Daxis can switch access mode to proxy or server_fallback for the affected segment.

externalItems.summary: Daxis production dashboards expose browser execution, fallback/block reasons, data fetch/object access, and worker health.
requiredProofArtifacts: Sample dashboard panels for the first rollout segment
acceptanceChecks: Dashboards break down by tenant, workspace, table class, runtime SKU, release channel, and browser family
Dashboards preserve fallback and block reasons
Dashboards correlate browser bytes with Daxis access evidence
Dashboards expose worker startup and terminal errors
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Dashboard includes a segment-level view showing server_fallback after rollback.

externalItems.summary: Daxis production runbooks cover resolver failure, object-grant failure, CORS failure, worker startup failure, and elevated fallback rates.
acceptanceChecks: Each runbook has owner, symptoms, first checks, rollback, and escalation path
Runbooks preserve request and correlation IDs during triage
Runbooks distinguish Daxis service failures from Axon runtime failures
Runbooks include fallback or kill-switch instructions
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Runbook drill proves Daxis can force server_fallback without a new Axon release.

externalItems.summary: Daxis rollout controls support tenant, workspace, table class, runtime SKU, release channel, and browser-family segmentation.
requiredProofArtifacts: Segment promotion and rollback audit log
acceptanceChecks: Rollout can move a segment through disabled, server_fallback, descriptor_only, brokered_grants, browser_datafusion, and stable_default
Kill switch can force server_fallback by segment
Rollout state is visible in telemetry
Rollback preserves user state and fallback reasons
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Kill-switch test proves affected tenants or workspaces return to server_fallback.

externalItems.owner: Daxis catalog platform and QA / performance engineering
externalItems.summary: Daxis production compatibility dashboard inventories current tables against Axon browser support.
requiredProofArtifacts: Last Axon release and query corpus result per rollout segment
acceptanceChecks: Dashboard includes tenant, workspace, table, table class, Delta protocol, Delta features, policy decision, access mode, browser compatibility, fallback reason, and last verification time
Dashboard separates production candidates from policy-blocked, server-only, proxy-only, unknown-feature, and unsupported SQL/table cases
Dashboard is refreshed before candidate and stable promotions
Dashboard can explain why each table is eligible, fallback-only, or blocked
externalItems.rollbackEvidence: Compatibility dashboard identifies affected tables before and after rollback.

handoffChecklist: Attach Daxis service endpoint test results.
Attach Daxis dashboard and oncall proof.
